title: viola guadalupensis
intro: Viola guadalupensis, also known as the Guadalupe violet or Purple prairie violet, is a species of flowering plant in the violet family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can be used to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Viola guadalupensis include the relief of pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Viola guadalupensis can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the plant to make a capsule or tincture.
